results.title: Poppi Review (Best Poppi Flavor, Ranked) - Vitality Vixens
results.author: Erin Zack
results.text:
Poppi Review (Best Poppi Flavor, Ranked) - Vitality Vixens

Throughout the past few weeks, my friend Cassi and I have been trying all of the flavors of Poppi (the ones we could find anyway). In this Poppi review, we’ll be sharing our brutally honest opinions on all the Poppi flavors, ranking them from worst (never buy again) to best (absolutely obsessed, in love, head over heels). I’ll also be answering a few questions like: 1) Is Poppi good for you? 2) Is Poppi or Olipop healthier?

## Poppi Review

This honest Poppi review will be broken up into the following sections (click the section to jump to it).

- Is Poppi good for you?
- Is Olipop or Poppi healthier?
- Ranking of Worst to Best Poppi Flavors

This review is unsponsored and all of our honest opinions. Of course, our opinions on the flavors may differ from yours, so I’d recommend trying out the flavors yourself! You can get a variety pack with a bunch of different flavors on Amazon, which is a great way to test them out and see what you like.

- Classic Sodas variety pack
- Fruity variety pack

Let’s get into the questions on if Poppi is healthy…

## Is the drink Poppi good for you?

Poppi is advertised as a healthier alternative to traditional soda. If you look at the Nutrition Facts and ingredients list, you’ll see why.

Looking at the Poppi Nutrition Facts for the classic cola, it has 25 calories and 5 grams of sugar. Compare this to a can of classic Coke which contains 140 calories and 39 grams of sugar.

However, you could look at a Poppi compared to a diet soda and think, this doesn’t seem much healthier. If you look at the nutrition facts on a diet Coke, you’ll see 0 calories and 0 grams of sugar. So from a strictly numbers perspective, the Poppi doesn’t seem any better, right?

However, you’ll want to look a little closer at the ingredients to really determine this.

##### Sweetener:

As opposed to traditional diet soda that are artificially sweetened, Poppi is sweetened with organic cane sugar and Stevia.

##### Prebiotics:

Poppi’s main claim to nutritional fame is that it’s a prebiotic soda.

If you’ve heard of probiotic foods (food that contain healthy bacteria, such as yogurt, kombucha, and fermented foods), this will help you understand what prebiotics are.

Prebiotics are basically food for these healthy gut bacteria. So to have a healthy gut/microbiome, you’d ideally want to have both prebiotics and probiotics in your diet.

Poppi also contains apple cider vinegar.

Overall, Poppi does seem to be a healthier alternative to traditional soda. But as always, it’s better to get prebiotics (and probiotics, and any other vitamins/minerals, for that matter) from a well balanced diet.

So while Poppi is a great option if you’re looking to cut back on soda, or just want a sweet treat in your day, you should absolutely still be eating a well rounded diet including fruits, vegetables, etc., for real nutritional benefits.

## Is Olipop or Poppi healthier?

Olipop is another popular pr

results.title: I Tried Every Flavor of Poppi I Could Find, Ranked From Worst to Best.
results.author: Ted Berg
results.text:
I Tried Every Flavor of Poppi I Could Find, Ranked From Worst to Best. - Business Insider 

Review by Ted Berg

I tried nine different flavors of Poppi prebiotic soda. Ted Berg

2024-04-22T12:22:01.537Z

Share

Copy link Email Facebook WhatsApp X LinkedIn Bluesky Threads

Save Saved Read in app

This story is available exclusively to Business Insider subscribers. Become an Insider and start reading now.

- I tried all the flavors of Poppi prebiotic soda I could find and ranked them from worst to best.
- I was disappointed by a few of the flavors, like watermelon and strawberry-lemon.
- Of all the flavors I tried, the ginger lime was my favorite.

It's no secret that prebiotic sodas have grown in popularity over the past couple of years.

In 2023, sales soared for popular brands like Poppi, which gained attention in 2018 after sealing a deal on "Shark Tank."

These carbonated drinks, which contain plant fibers that help good bacteria grow and thrive, claim to support things like gut health and blood-sugar regulation.

Still, I wanted to see if these sodas lived up to the hype. On a recent trip to my local supermarket, I bought all the Poppi prebiotic- soda flavors I could find.

Each 12-ounce can of soda contains under 25 calories, which is significantly less than what you'd find in the same-size serving of Coke or Fanta.

Here's my ranking of the flavors I tried, from worst to best.

## Watermelon was easily my least favorite flavor.

I found that Poppi's watermelon flavor tasted medicinal. Ted Berg

Although it wasn't easy picking my favorite Poppi flavor, I had no trouble selecting my least favorite.

I typically enjoy watermelon-flavored things (it's my go-to Blow Pop flavor, for example), but Poppi's effort at it tasted medicinal to me.

I thought it tasted like the watermelon-flavored toothpaste that hung around in our medicine cabinet for months after my kids rejected it.

## I was disappointed by the strawberry-lemon flavor.

I could make out the distinct tastes of both strawberry and lemon. Ted Berg

Strawberry lemon sounded like a great idea for a soda flavor. When I took a sip, I could taste the distinct flavors of both strawberry and lemon.

However, something about this flavor tasted too artificial to me — especially in the aftertaste. It reminded me of the bubblegum that used to come with packs of baseball cards.

## Raspberry rose was too floral for me.

I didn't love Poppi's raspberry-rose flavor. Ted Berg

I found many of Poppi's most creative flavors to be disappointing, and raspberry rose was no exception.

It had a nice, crisp raspberry flavor, but it was overwhelmed by a perfumy floral taste that became increasingly unpleasant as I drank more.

I wouldn't be surprised if someone handed me something that tasted just like this at the cocktail hour of an outdoor wedding. However, I'd probably only take a few sips before ditching it on an empty table somewhere.

## Poppi's root-beer flavor was fine — but I'd prefer the traditional

results.title: All Poppi flavors ranked worst to best | The Mary Sue
results.author: Sarah Fimm
results.text:
All Poppi flavors ranked worst to best | The Mary Sue

### 10 Movies that Predicted the Future

### Ohio man does scan-and-go at Sam’s Club. Then he checks his bank account: ‘Chase Bank, do better’

### Pittsburgh woman mortifies the guy she liked after she makes a hilarious shaving decision before meeting him: ‘learn from my mistakes’

### What Did Jimmy Kimmel Do to Offend the Trumps This Time?

### Texas man takes woman to Applebee’s on a date. Then he sees what she orders: ‘A wine bottle fell out of her back pocket’

# All Poppi flavors ranked worst to best

By Sarah Fimm Oct 8th, 2024, 2:02 pm

I’m not sure where the competition is worse, the Olympics or the soft drink industry. Old school brands like Coca Cola have been crushing competition since 1886. How’s another soda supposed to compete? Despite being around for a hundred years less, Poppi manages. Here are all the flavors, ranked.

Recommended Videos

## 12. Watermelon

(Poppi)

Drinking Watermelon flavored Poppi feels kinda like licking the bottom of a bag of Sour Patch kids. The ghost of better tasting flavors lingers there on the plastic, but it’s not exactly satisfying to run your tongue across. There’s a reason why classic soda brands have no truck with watermelons. The real thing is great on a summer day, but when you try to synthesize the flavor, you’re left with something akin to a Frankenstein monster of fruit.

## 11. Classic Cola

(Poppi)

Stay in your lane, Poppi. There’s nothing classic about you, and that’s okay. You’re only four years old. You’re a “healthy” soda. You weren’t once made with literal cocaine like a certain soda brand from which this particular flavor takes its unfortunate inspiration. While Poppi’s fruity flavors are refreshing, the regular cola version is a far cry from the sophisticated taste of established soda brands who had a century to get the recepie right.

## 10. Wild Berry

(Poppi)

Wild Berry how could you let me down like this? Your blue can promises a taste of the most glorious of all manmade flavors: blue raspberry. But alas, the promise was broken once the liquid touched my lips. Rather than a blue raspberry Jolly Rancher, Wild Berry tastes like someone whispered the word “blue raspberry” over a sugar coated plastic bag. Don’t get me wrong, I’ll still drink it. But begrudgingly.

## 9. Lemon Lime

(Poppi)

Pride goeth before the fall. Poppi, baby, how could you possible be so vain, so arrogant, so full of hubris to think that your Lemon Lime could compete with the ambrosian majesty of Sprite? This flavor tastes like the wax of Icarus’ wings as they melted in the cruel heat of the sun, but with a hint of lemon.

## 8. Orange

(Poppi)

Poppi’s Orange flavored soda is serviceable but it would not be enough to sway connoisseurs like Keenan and Kel away from the real thing. You’re like a lip lock person who drank a bottle of Sunny D. The taste lingers, but like reading a book originally penned in another language, the result is like kissing throu

results.title: The Ultimate Poppi Review: A Healthier Soda
results.text:
-- The Ultimate Poppi Review: A Healthier Soda

top of page

Search

A common New Year’s resolution is the desire to be healthier in one’s daily life. Health trends are very popular on social media as people love sharing and finding little tips and lifestyles that help them to prioritize their health. One of the most popular trends in recent years has been taking foods that a person typically consumes and swapping them out for healthier alternatives that taste similar. From small snacks to full meals, if there’s a food you like, there’s an influencer on social media with a “healthier” version of it. One of the most popular alternatives in recent years has been with soda. Although many well-known soda brands have diet versions of their drinks, many people are still not satisfied with the options presented to them, so they may choose to search for brands that specialize in creating healthier sodas. Poppi is a soda company that creates soda with probiotics and apple cider vinegar. The brand has been on Shark Tank and has been featured by influencers and celebrities, but is it really as good as normal soda? We’re here to find out.

As loyal Whole Foods and Sprouts shoppers, we are no strangers to all sorts of healthy alternatives and probiotic drinks. Seeing the recent rise in Poppi’s popularity, we set out to review these fizzy drinks that promised to be oh-so-good for our gut health. And yes, we looked like maniacs carrying every flavor to the checkout line at the grocery store. It was worth it though, for now we are qualified Poppi experts. However, our stomachs may have actually hurt more than before after drinking this much soda (how ironic!).... Here are our reviews of the fourteen flavors, as well as a review of the “ultimate” Poppi.

Photo provided by authors.

Photo provided by authors.

Strawberry Lemon (7.66/10)

This flavor is absolutely delicious. It tastes just like strawberry lemonade with a fizzy twist and does not taste like a health drink at all. We all drank strawberry lemonade as kids, which makes this Poppi perfect to drink if you’re feeling a bit nostalgic for the classic drink.

Orange (7/10)

Orange was good, but didn’t stand out as great. It tastes just like a Fanta with less bubbles and sugar in it. I would rather just drink a Fanta, honestly.

Raspberry Rose (9/10)

This flavor is definitely the best. First of all, it’s just so cute– the baby pink, smell of rose, and gentle taste of raspberry. It’s also perfectly romantic and makes one happy to be drinking it. As raspberry enthusiasts, we gladly report that the flavor of raspberry itself is spot on, almost reminiscent of real raspberry.

Wild Berry (8/10)

Wild berry is most well-known for being featured in Poppi’s partnership with Post Malone and it is certainly deserving of this spotlight. The only flaw of this drink would be its sweetness, but that also makes it taste more like regular soda. It tastes very similar to a Blue Raspberry Slurpee at the movie theatres; so if

results.title: I Tried More Than a Dozen Poppi Flavors and Found the 10 Best
results.author: Jordan Myrick  and   Gwynedd Stuart
results.text:
I Tried More Than a Dozen Poppi Flavors and Found the 10 Best

# The 10 Best Poppi Flavors, According to Taste Testers Who Tried Them All

Credit: Liv Averett / Instacart / Target

Copy this link to share with your friends!

`
 https://sporked.com/article/best-poppi-flavors/ `

The best prebiotic sodas taste sweet and satisfying, not like watery vinegar. By this standard, Poppi rules. We’ve tried a lot of prebiotic soda, and Poppi is one of a small handful of brands with more hits than misses.

## Videos by Sporked

To find the very best Poppi flavors, we tried them all. Twice. Our first taste test was back in 2023 and there are way more Poppi flavors now—good ones! We’re a little skeptical of the actual health benefits of prebiotic sodas, but we can’t deny that Poppi tastes good either way. If you’re on the prebiotic soda train, check out this list of the best Poppi flavors.

The following article contains affiliate links that may generate a small commission to us when you make a purchase through the link. Learn more about how we work with affiliates here.

Poppi!

Root Beer

Poppi root beer genuinely tastes like root beer. That should go without saying, but in the prebiotic soda world, it simply doesn’t. It has a nice, zippy sassafras flavor that covers the flavor of the stevia sweetener (yep, I’m sorry to report that Poppi is sweetened with stevia leaf). Put this in a frosty mug and you’ll be extremely happy enjoying it with a burger. —Gwynedd Stuart

Credit: Merc / Poppi

Rating:

7.5/10

Sporks

Poppi!

Orange

Orange Poppi has an excellent, genuine orange flavor. It’s very nice and simple. It’s like if orange soda was subtle. We like orange cream better, but we’ll get to that in a sec… —Jordan Myrick

Credit: Merc / Poppi

Rating:

8/10

Sporks

### Poppi!

Poppi Cream Soda

There’s something about the creamy Poppi flavors that just works. This has a mellow, lovely vanilla cream flavor that blends nicely with the alternative sweeteners in the mix. You expect cream soda to be sweet and stevia is SWEET, so it just makes sense. —Gwynedd Stuart

Credit: Liv Averett / Instacart

Rating:

8/10

Sporks

### Poppi!

Poppi Cherry Cola

We REALLY didn’t like Poppi’s regular cola—prebiotic colas generally kind of suck—but if you’re craving a classic soda and want low sugar and some bacteria in the mix, this is the best Poppi flavor to try. The key is that this doesn’t really taste like cola, it tastes more like a black cherry soda. It works! —Gwynedd Stuart

Credit: Liv Averett / Instacart

Rating:

8.5/10

Sporks

### Poppi!

Poppi Cranberry Fizz

This seasonal Poppi flavor tastes like Ocean Spray Cranberry Juice Cocktail but carbonated. Okay, it tastes like a diet cranberry juice cocktail, but all the same it has that nice tartness and subtle tannic sort of flavor you want from a cranberry-flavored bev. —Gwynedd Stuart

Credit: Liv Averett / Instacart

Rating:

8.5/10

Sporks

### Poppi!

Poppi Grape

This is just a really good diet grape soda with

results.title: Best Poppi Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Complete Taste Test Guide
results.author: RON SILBERSTEIN
results.text:
Best Poppi Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Complete Taste Test Guide

If you’re looking for the best Poppi flavors ranked by people who actually drink them, you’ve come to the right place. Our team spent weeks testing every single Poppi flavor on the market, tasting them side-by-side, and debating which cans deserve a spot in your fridge.

Poppi has taken the soda world by storm since launching in 2018. This prebiotic soda combines agave inulin, cassava root fiber, apple cider vinegar, and stevia to create a gut-healthy alternative to traditional soda. But let’s be honest, if it doesn’t taste good, the health benefits don’t matter much.

After tasting all 17 current flavors multiple times, we’ve developed a definitive ranking. We considered flavor authenticity, sweetness balance, carbonation level, aftertaste, and how each compares to its traditional soda counterpart. The results might surprise you.

Table of Contents

Toggle

## Best Poppi Flavors Ranked: Our Complete 2026 Tier List

Before we dive into individual flavors, here’s our tier system at a glance. This will help you quickly identify which flavors are must-tries and which you can probably skip.

- Final Thoughts on the Best Poppi Flavors
- S-Tier (Must Try): Wild Berry, Cherry Limeade, Raspberry Rose
- A-Tier (Excellent): Orange, Doc Pop, Classic Cola, Ginger Lime, Strawberry Lemon, Root Beer
- B-Tier (Good): Orange Cream, Grape, Cream Soda
- C-Tier (Average): Cranberry Fizz, Watermelon, Lemon Lime
- D-Tier (Skip): Variety Pack (inconsistent), Seasonal/Limited Edition (hit or miss)

Now let’s get into the detailed breakdown of each flavor, starting from the bottom and working our way to the absolute best Poppi has to offer.

## D-Tier: The Flavors to Skip

### Seasonal and Limited Edition Flavors

Poppi releases seasonal flavors throughout the year, and honestly, they’re experimental at best. We’ve tried Pumpkin Spice in fall, Peppermint during winter, and various fruit blends in summer. The problem is inconsistency. Some are decent, others are undrinkable.

The issue with seasonal flavors is they often lean too heavily into artificial extracts rather than real fruit flavors. The pumpkin version tasted like spice rack water with an unsettling stevia aftertaste. Unless you’re a die-hard Poppi collector, skip the limited releases and stick to the core lineup.

### C-Tier: Middle of the Pack

#### Lemon Lime Poppi

Lemon Lime suffers from an identity crisis. Is it trying to be Sprite? 7-Up? Something else entirely? The flavor profile is generic citrus without the bright, crisp notes that make those classic sodas refreshing. It’s not bad, just forgettable.

The carbonation feels slightly flatter than other Poppi flavors, and the stevia aftertaste is more pronounced. If you want a citrus Poppi, the Orange or Ginger Lime will serve you much better. Lemon Lime works in a pinch, but it won’t convert any soda skeptics.

#### Watermelon Poppi

Watermelon was one of our most anticipated flavors, and

results.title: Poppi Review – Spreading The Love
results.author: Madisyn Weiss
results.text:
Poppi Review – Spreading The Love

# Poppi Review

Hi, this is Madisyn and today I’m going to be reviewing a bunch of flavors from my favorite type of drink which is called “Poppi”.

Poppi is a prebiotic soda and has no caffeine which is a good thing for me since I don’t like caffeine and it makes my tremors worse. I also get headaches from it too. So I prefer drinking Poppi instead and it also helps with my anxiety and stomach aches.

I tried a bunch of flavors and here is my review on them.

Orange:

It tastes like a better version of Orange Fanta if you don’t want the caffeine.

If you love fruity flavor lollipops and then this flavor is for you.

When you put it into a cup or drink it out of the can it looks like a sparkling drink with magic.

The smell is like when you peel and eat an orange and your hands start smelling citrusy.

Grape:

To me it looks like a healthier version of wine.

If you enjoy watching videos of wineries and then this flavor will give you that experience.

Classic Cola:

This drink looks like beer if you look at it closely.

It also smells fresh and delicious.

Not to bubbly which is a good thing so it doesn’t spill over.

A less strong coke which is good for the pallet.

Raspberry Rose:

Looks like Pink lemonade if you enjoy getting a sweet refreshment.

Smells like Raspberry lemonade and tastes like it too.

Raspberry Rose reminds me of walking through a field of fresh flowers.

Rootbeer:

Smells strong and like going into a tavern or bar.

Also has a spicy and cinnamon taste.

Reminds me of a spice rack you have in your kitchen.

Doc Pop:

Smells sweet.

Reminds me of medicine.

Remember when you were little and you didn’t want to take your medicine, so you would run away from your parents until they bribed you to take your medicine.

Watermelon:

Smells like Watermelon candy.

Tastes like lemonade.

Imagine having lemonade on a spring or summer day.

Wild berry:

This is my favorite flavor.

Tastes like a blueberry slushy or if you prefer a snow cone.

When you pour it into a cup it looks purpley pink with a hint of blue.

To me it tastes like happiness.

## Discover more from Spreading The Love

Subscribe now to keep reading and get access to the full archive.

results.title: Every Poppi Soda Flavor, Ranked - Tasting Table
results.text:
Every Poppi Soda Flavor, Ranked

# Every Poppi Soda Flavor, Ranked

By Julia Collins Updated: March 27, 2025 11:43 am EST

Julia Collins/Tasting Table

If you haven't heard of Poppi by now, we're pretty surprised, to say the least. One of the trendiest brands that seem to be in many major grocery stores at the moment, the prebiotic soda drink brand is blowing people away with its tastiness and supposed health benefits.

Poppi, originally called Mother, actually appeared on "Shark Tank" a few years ago, and though not all "Shark Tank" businesses take off, this one did. If you're curious about what a prebiotic soda is, it's basically a "healthier" carbonated drink that tastes very similar to soda without the extra sugar — but, more importantly, it contains apple cider vinegar, a natural prebiotic. It's also flavored with natural fruit juice concentrates and is often compared to another Olipop, another soda brand that touts digestive and other health benefits. Some Poppi flavors have come and gone, but there are currently nine on the market. We decided to put those flavors to the test to see which one came out on top as our favorite. Spoiler alert: There was some stiff competition for the No. 1 spot.

## 14. Root Beer

Julia Collins/Tasting Table

If you are a fan of classic soda that tastes like it has been watered down to the point where you can barely recognize it, this is the flavor for you. Now, don't get us wrong, we are big fans of Poppi (as you'll see for some of the higher-ranking flavors), but we're also pretty big fans of root beer. And when it's good, it's good. But when it's bad, well, you should probably skip it.

Poppi's root beer flavor earned last place in our ranking because it missed the mark on quality and flavor. It did have the classic root beer taste, but it just wasn't strong enough to be considered the real deal. Long story short, it tasted pretty akin to watered-down root beer, which we found especially disappointing considering our initial excitement over the flavor's existence. Do yourself a favor and skip this flavor. Instead, you might want to consider one of the best root beer brands we've tried before.

## 13. Doc Pop

Julia Collins/Tasting Table

With Doc Pop from Poppi, we felt bamboozled. On the first sip, we assumed it was grape-flavored soda. It tasted identical, actually. So when we discovered it was supposed to be cherry and vanilla-flavored soda (in a similar vein to Dr Pepper), we were a bit shell-shocked. That being said, this prebiotic soda actually tastes identical to traditional grape soda. If you poured a regular grape soda and the Poppi Doc Pop flavor in two cups and didn't tell us which was which, we wouldn't be able to tell them apart. It's pretty impressive. So even though it didn't nail cherry and vanilla soda, it did accidentally hit the mark with grape. However, we aren't exactly grape-flavored soda enthusiasts. (Though we can completely recognize that this flavor is miles ahead of Poppi's root be
